message.content: Adversaries may exploit the LSASS Driver to gain persistence on compromised systems by modifying or adding malicious drivers. The Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS) is crucial for managing local security policies and user authentication in Windows environments. By targeting LSASS drivers, adversaries can ensure continuous execution of malicious payloads. Common methods include replacing legitimate drivers or adding illegitimate ones, as seen in malware like Pasam and Wingbird. Pasam infects the Security Accounts Manager (SAM) DLL to load a malicious DLL, while Wingbird registers a service to load a malicious driver, executing its payload before causing system instability. The impact of such exploitation includes persistent unauthorized access and potential system instability. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring and validating driver integrity, employing endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions, and ensuring robust logging and alerting mechanisms to detect suspicious driver-related activities.
